project: COPY ANUNCIOS — Edge Cases Fase 2 (Alta Severidade)
description: Fix 13 high-severity edge cases: DimensionForm source map, retry/race fixes, deduplication, token refresh lock, permission filtering, auth hardening, and last admin protection.

userStories.title: Pass user_id in copy_with_dimensions for audit logs
userStories.description: As an admin, I want all copy operations to record who executed them so I have complete audit trails.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/services/item_copier.py: add user_id parameter to copy_with_dimensions function signature (default None)
In copy_with_dimensions: propagate user_id to each call of copy_single_item
In app/routers/copy.py POST /api/copy/with-dimensions endpoint: pass user['id'] as user_id to copy_with_dimensions
In app/routers/copy.py POST /api/copy/retry-dimensions endpoint: pass user['id'] as user_id to copy_with_dimensions
Verify that copy_logs entries created by copy_with_dimensions include user_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/services/item_copier.py: copy_with_dimensions is around line 1020-1040. It calls copy_single_item which already accepts user_id. Just add the parameter and pass it through. In app/routers/copy.py: the with-dimensions endpoint is around line 200-240, retry-dimensions around line 240-260. Both have access to user dict from require_active_org dependency. Simple parameter threading.

userStories.title: Fix retry-dimensions to use org_id from original log
userStories.description: As a super admin, I want retry-dimensions to use the org_id from the original log so the operation works correctly when I retry logs from other organizations.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/routers/copy.py retry-dimensions endpoint: after fetching the log row, extract log_org_id = log['org_id']
Use log_org_id (not user['org_id']) when calling copy_with_dimensions
Use log_org_id (not user['org_id']) when updating the copy_logs entry after retry
Super admin can still access the log (existing behavior — super admin skips org filter)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/copy.py: the retry-dimensions endpoint (~line 241) calls copy_with_dimensions with org_id from the user session. For super_admin, this is the super_admin's org, not the original log's org. The fix: use log['org_id'] from the fetched copy_log row instead. The log query already fetches the row (check around line 245-250). Also update the log update query to use log_org_id for the .eq('org_id', ...) filter.

userStories.title: Deduplicate item IDs in frontend and backend
userStories.description: As an operator, I want duplicate IDs to be automatically removed so I don't trigger redundant API calls or duplicate copies.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/components/CopyForm.tsx normalizeAndResolve: after splitting and normalizing IDs, deduplicate them preserving order
Show a small info message below the textarea when duplicates were removed: '{N} duplicata(s) removida(s)' styled with color var(--ink-faint), fontSize var(--text-xs)
The textarea content is updated to show only unique IDs
In app/routers/copy.py copy endpoint: after cleaning item_ids, apply clean_ids = list(dict.fromkeys(clean_ids))
In app/routers/copy.py resolve-sellers endpoint: deduplicate item_ids before processing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Frontend: in CopyForm.tsx normalizeAndResolve (~line 101-106), after const ids = normalized.split('\n').filter(Boolean), add dedup: const unique = [...new Set(ids)], track removed count, update textarea with unique.join('\n'). Add a state variable for the dedup message. Backend: in app/routers/copy.py, the copy endpoint cleans IDs around line 112. Add clean_ids = list(dict.fromkeys(clean_ids)) after the existing cleaning. Same for resolve_sellers_endpoint around line 390-408.

userStories.title: Allow retry of resolve after network failure
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to be able to re-paste the same IDs after a network failure without clearing the textarea first.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/components/CopyForm.tsx resolveAll: move lastResolvedKey.current = key to AFTER setResolvedSources (inside the success path)
On error (catch block), lastResolvedKey.current is NOT updated so same IDs can be retried
Add a 'Tentar novamente' button that appears when resolveError is set
The retry button clears lastResolvedKey.current and calls normalizeAndResolve
Button styled as btn-ghost with fontSize var(--text-xs), placed next to the error message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In CopyForm.tsx: resolveAll (~line 51-99) sets lastResolvedKey.current = key at line 55, BEFORE the fetch. Move it to after setResolvedSources(sources) around line 88. In the catch block, do NOT set lastResolvedKey. For the retry button: add it next to the resolveError paragraph (~line 206-210). On click: lastResolvedKey.current = ''; normalizeAndResolve().

userStories.title: Prevent race condition between paste and blur resolve
userStories.description: As an operator, I want pasting IDs and pressing Tab quickly to not cause duplicate resolve requests.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/components/CopyForm.tsx: add an AbortController ref to cancel in-flight resolve requests when a new one starts
In resolveAll: if there is an existing AbortController, abort it before starting a new request
Create a new AbortController, pass its signal to the fetch call
On abort (AbortError), do not set resolveError — silently ignore
The final result always reflects the last triggered resolve, not an earlier one
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In CopyForm.tsx: add const abortRef = useRef<AbortController | null>(null). In resolveAll: at the top, if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort(); then abortRef.current = new AbortController(). Pass { signal: abortRef.current.signal } to the fetch options (merge with existing headers). In the catch block, check if error is AbortError: if (e instanceof DOMException && e.name === 'AbortError') return; — otherwise set resolveError normally.

userStories.title: Disable destination selection during resolve
userStories.description: As an operator, I want destination selection to be disabled while seller detection is running so I don't select invalid destinations.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/components/CopyForm.tsx: the 'Selecionar todos' button is disabled when resolving === true
Individual destination chip buttons are disabled (not clickable, visually dimmed) when resolving === true
Add opacity: 0.5 and pointerEvents: 'none' to chips during resolving
After resolve completes, destinations that now conflict with source sellers are automatically removed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In CopyForm.tsx: the 'Selecionar todos' button is around line 286-293. Add disabled={resolving} prop. The chip buttons are around line 298-316. Add disabled={resolving} and conditional style. For auto-cleanup of conflicting destinations: in the resolveAll success path (after setResolvedSources), the validDests is already computed from resolved sources. Destinations state is already cleared on line 60 (setDestinations([])), so this may already work. Verify the flow.

userStories.title: Invalidate all sessions after password reset
userStories.description: As a user, I want all my sessions invalidated when I reset my password so that a stolen token loses access.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/routers/auth.py reset-password endpoint: after updating the password hash, delete ALL user_sessions for that user_id
Use db.table('user_sessions').delete().eq('user_id', user_id).execute()
Also delete all password_reset_tokens for the same user (not just the used token)
The user must log in again after resetting their password
Log the action in auth_logs with action 'password_reset_sessions_cleared'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/auth.py: the reset-password endpoint is around line 382-388. After the password update query, add the session deletion query. The password_reset_tokens table stores tokens — delete all for the user, not just the used one. The auth_logs insert follows the existing pattern in the file (check login/logout handlers for the format).

userStories.title: Remove username login — require email only
userStories.description: As a platform admin, I want login to require email (not username) to prevent cross-tenant username collisions.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/routers/auth.py login endpoint: remove the fallback that tries to find user by username
Login only accepts email field — search users table by email only
If user not found by email, return 401 'Email ou senha incorretos'
Update the LoginRequest model: rename field to 'email' if it's currently 'username' or 'login'
Frontend login form (frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx): update label and placeholder to say 'Email' instead of 'Email ou usuario'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/auth.py: the login endpoint (~line 150-153) first tries email match, then falls back to username match. Remove the username fallback entirely. The LoginRequest model has a field (check if it's called 'login', 'email', or 'username'). Rename to 'email' with type EmailStr if not already. In the frontend: LoginPage.tsx has the login form — update the input label and placeholder. This is a breaking change for users who log in by username, but the user explicitly decided to remove this.

userStories.title: Prevent deleting or demoting last admin of org
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the system to prevent the organization from having zero admins so there is always someone with control.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/routers/admin_users.py DELETE user endpoint: before deleting, if user is admin, check if there's at least 1 other active admin in the same org
In app/routers/admin_users.py PUT user endpoint: before changing role from admin to operator, same check
In app/routers/admin_users.py PUT user endpoint: before setting active=false on an admin, same check
If would leave org with zero admins: return 400 'Nao e possivel remover o ultimo administrador da organizacao'
Self-delete and self-demote allowed only if another admin exists
Query: SELECT count(*) FROM users WHERE org_id = X AND role = 'admin' AND active = true AND id != target_user_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/admin_users.py: the DELETE endpoint and PUT endpoint handle user management. Before any operation that would remove an admin (delete, change role to operator, set active=false), query the users table to count other active admins. Use: db.table('users').select('id', count='exact').eq('org_id', org_id).eq('role', 'admin').eq('active', True).neq('id', target_user_id).execute(). If count == 0, reject with 400.

userStories.title: Token refresh lock per seller to prevent disconnection
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the system to use a per-seller lock when refreshing ML tokens so concurrent requests don't invalidate the refresh token and disconnect the seller.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/services/ml_api.py: create a module-level dict _token_locks: dict[str, asyncio.Lock] for per-seller locks
Add a helper function _get_seller_lock(seller_slug) that returns or creates a lock for the seller
In _get_token: acquire the seller lock before checking/refreshing the token
After acquiring the lock, re-check if token was already refreshed by another coroutine (compare ml_token_expires_at with current time)
If already refreshed, use the new token without calling ML API again
Use asyncio.wait_for with timeout=30 seconds to prevent deadlocks
Existing sellers continue working — no re-auth needed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/services/ml_api.py: _get_token is the function that checks token expiry and refreshes if needed. It's called from multiple concurrent requests. The fix: _token_locks = {} at module level. def _get_seller_lock(slug): if slug not in _token_locks: _token_locks[slug] = asyncio.Lock(); return _token_locks[slug]. In _get_token: async with await asyncio.wait_for(_get_seller_lock(slug).acquire(), timeout=30): re-read token from DB, check if still expired, only then do the refresh. Use try/finally to release lock. With 1 uvicorn worker in Docker, this handles all concurrent requests within the process.

userStories.title: Filter resolve-sellers results by can_copy_from permission
userStories.description: As an admin, I want resolve-sellers to only return sellers the operator has can_copy_from permission for, preventing information leakage about item ownership.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/routers/copy.py resolve_sellers_endpoint: after resolving sellers, filter results based on user permissions
For non-admin users: only include results where the user has can_copy_from for that seller_slug
Items whose seller the operator lacks permission for go to the errors list with message 'Sem permissao para este seller'
Admin users see all results (bypass permission check)
Use the same permission check pattern as the copy endpoint (_check_seller_permission or direct query)
The frontend already filters client-side (CopyForm.tsx:81-86) but the backend must be the source of truth
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/copy.py: resolve_sellers_endpoint is around line 390-408. It uses require_active_org which gives the user dict. After resolving items to sellers, check permissions: query user_permissions for the user's can_copy_from permissions. If user.role != 'admin', filter results. Move items with unauthorized sellers to the errors array. The _check_seller_permission helper (if refactored to shared module in US-008 Fase 1) can be reused, otherwise query user_permissions directly.

userStories.title: DimensionForm uses correct source seller per item
userStories.description: As an operator, I want dimension retry to use the correct source seller when I copied items from multiple sellers, so the correction actually works.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/pages/CopyPage.tsx: build a sourceMap (Record<string, string> mapping item_id to seller_slug) from copy groups and pass it to CopyProgress
In frontend/src/components/CopyProgress.tsx: accept sourceMap prop instead of single source string
In CopyProgress handleDimensionSubmit: look up the correct source seller from sourceMap using the item_id
The POST /api/copy/with-dimensions request sends the correct source for each item
When copying from SellerA and SellerB, retry for SellerB items uses SellerB as source (not SellerA)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In CopyPage.tsx: when building groups in handleCopy (~line 73-118), also build a sourceMap from the groups: const sourceMap: Record<string, string> = {}; groups.forEach(g => g.itemIds.forEach(id => sourceMap[id] = g.source)). Pass sourceMap to CopyProgress (currently passes source as a single string via results.source). In CopyProgress.tsx: the component receives results with .source. Change to accept sourceMap. In handleDimensionSubmit, use sourceMap[itemId] instead of the single source. CopyProgress.tsx is in frontend/src/components/.

userStories.title: Limit SKUs in compat search endpoint
userStories.description: As an admin, I want SKU search to be limited to 50 per request to prevent API overload.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/routers/compat.py SearchSkuRequest model: add Pydantic validator to limit skus to max 50
If exceeded: raise validation error 'Maximo de 50 SKUs por busca'
In the search-sku endpoint: add asyncio.Semaphore(10) to limit concurrent ML API requests
Frontend compat form: show SKU count and a warning when approaching 50
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/compat.py: SearchSkuRequest has skus: List[str]. Add @field_validator('skus') to check len(v) <= 50. For the semaphore: in the search-sku handler, create sem = asyncio.Semaphore(10) and wrap each ML API call task with async with sem. The frontend compat page has a SKU textarea — add a counter showing '{count}/50 SKUs' and warning styling when count > 45.
